title: NJ Talingting
text:
I build open-source tools in R to resolve the “Continental Fallacy”— the geographic bias that treats our oceans as barriers rather than vital conductors of connectivity in spatial modeling.
While studying Statistics in Cebu— situated beside the Philippines’ busiest maritime hub— I witnessed the gap between logistical reality and spatial data. Since 2023, I have balanced my duties as an elected public official with the development of Research Software Engineering (RSE) tools that translate these maritime flows into reproducible science. My packages are indexed on the Comprehensive R Archive Network (CRAN).
Beyond code, I lead and maintain PinasR, a centralized registry for Filipino-led R development. We aspire to cultivate a national culture of open science and sustainable software, ensuring the Philippines has a seat at the table in the global RSE community.
Explore the tools I’ve built to solve these challenges under the Software section!
